chapters.label: Chapter 1 — Syntax
chapters.caption: Programs whose syntax we read or parse.

entries.title: Integer division — m / n
entries.blurb: Repeated subtraction. d ends with the quotient, r with the remainder.

entries.title: gcd via subtraction (Euclid)
entries.blurb: Repeatedly subtract the smaller from the larger; gcd lands in x when y = 0.

entries.title: Multiplication by repeated addition
entries.blurb: Multiply two numbers using only +, -, := (no * needed in the program).

entries.title: Power — m^n
entries.blurb: Exponentiation by repeated multiplication.

chapters.label: Chapter 2 — Small-step semantics
chapters.caption: Programs whose ⟨S, σ⟩ ⇒ ⟨S', σ'⟩ trace illustrates the rules.

entries.title: Integer square via repeated addition
entries.blurb: Compute n² using only +, := and a single while-loop.

entries.title: gcd by subtraction — long trace
entries.blurb: Same algorithm as Example 3, traced to demonstrate the chapter's table view.

entries.title: Counting bounded loop
entries.blurb: A short bounded loop chosen so the trace fits on one screen.

entries.title: Nested loop — pair counter
entries.blurb: Two nested while-loops; demonstrates trace structure on quadratic flow.

entries.title: Division — predict the trace
entries.blurb: Trace this division program by hand; check your answer here.

entries.title: Even-odd flag
entries.blurb: A program that walks an integer down and flips a flag — predict the trace.

entries.title: Parity check (is_odd)
entries.blurb: Set is_odd = 1 iff n is odd, using only +, -, :=, while.

entries.title: Counter with conditional
entries.blurb: A loop with a guarded if inside; predict the count.

entries.title: Multiplication — alternative form
entries.blurb: Same as Example 5 but a different loop structure; trace it.

entries.title: gcd — predict step count
entries.blurb: gcd by subtraction; predict total small-steps from m, n.

entries.title: Conditional with done flag
entries.blurb: Set done = 1 once a condition is reached; trace the unfolding.

chapters.label: Chapter 3 — Complexity
chapters.caption: Programs whose growth rate we measure with the step counter.

entries.title: Decrementing loop — Θ(n)
entries.blurb: Linear in n. Compare the step count for n = 5, 10, 20.

entries.title: Step count of a small program
entries.blurb: Predict the step count, then verify.

entries.title: Step count — alternative form
entries.blurb: Same task as Exercise 17, different loop. Same complexity?

entries.title: Quadratic — Θ(n²)
entries.blurb: Step count grows quadratically with i. Try i = 4, 8, 16.

entries.title: Constant program — Θ(1)
entries.blurb: Step count is independent of n. Measure to confirm.

entries.title: Linear — counting up
entries.blurb: Step count is linear; compute the constant factor.

entries.title: Three-variable counter
entries.blurb: Three nested-ish loops; what is the asymptotic class?

chapters.label: Chapter 4 — Hoare logic
chapters.caption: Programs we verify with {P} S {Q}. Switch to "Verify Hoare" tool.

entries.title: Conditional — verify postcondition
entries.blurb: A simple if/else; verify that the postcondition holds.

entries.title: Power — Hoare verification
entries.blurb: Verify a power program against {n ≥ 0} S {x = 2ⁿ}.

entries.title: Sum 1..n — verify n(n+1)/2
entries.blurb: Verify that sum 1..n satisfies x = n(n+1)/2.

entries.title: Integer log₂ via halving
entries.blurb: Verify that the program computes ⌊log₂ n⌋.

entries.title: Divisibility check
entries.blurb: Set d = 1 iff k divides n; verify against the obvious postcondition.

entries.title: Power — total correctness
entries.blurb: Same as Exercise 34, framed as total-correctness with a variant.

chapters.label: Chapter 5 — Computability
chapters.caption: Programs that decide or semidecide a predicate.

entries.title: Modulo 7 by repeated subtraction
entries.blurb: x ends with n mod 7. A bounded computation, easy to decide.

entries.title: Multiplication via repeated addition
entries.blurb: Decidable: for any (m, n) the program halts and gives m·n.

entries.title: Power — variant
entries.blurb: Another decidable program; halts on every input.

entries.title: Decide an odd predicate
entries.blurb: Check that the program correctly classifies n. Try n = 0, 1, 2, 7.

entries.title: Halting — bounded vs unbounded
entries.blurb: A program where halting depends on n. Try n = 100, 99, 50, 200.

chapters.label: Appendix — Big-step semantics
chapters.caption: Programs from the appendix exercises (small-step trace shown).

entries.title: Loop-free program — terminates
entries.blurb: Loop-free programs trivially terminate; trace and confirm.

entries.title: Equivalence under big-step
entries.blurb: A program for the big-step equivalence proof; trace its small-step run.

entries.title: Determinism showcase
entries.blurb: Big-step is deterministic: the same start gives the same end every time.

entries.title: gcd — big-step view
entries.blurb: gcd as a big-step terminating computation.

entries.title: Multiplication — big-step
entries.blurb: Multiplication; the result is the unique big-step answer.

entries.title: Big-step ↔ small-step agreement
entries.blurb: A program used in the proof that big-step and small-step agree.

chapters.label: CS Classics — extras
chapters.caption: Famous programs that aren't tied to a specific chapter exercise.

entries.title: sum 1..n
entries.blurb: The classic. result lands at n(n+1)/2.

entries.title: factorial — n!
entries.blurb: n! by repeated multiplication.

entries.title: Fibonacci — iterative
entries.blurb: Fibonacci computed iteratively. b ends with the (n+2)-th Fibonacci number.

entries.title: Collatz step counter
entries.blurb: Collatz: halve if even, 3x+1 if odd. Famously decidable in practice but unproven in general. (Halving expanded since While has no /.)

entries.title: Primality test — trial division
entries.blurb: Trial division up to ~√n. is_prime = 1 iff n is prime. Try 17, 21, 49.

entries.title: Diophantine search — find x, y with ax + by = 1
entries.blurb: Search for nonneg x, y solving ax + by = 1. Halts iff gcd(a,b) = 1.

entries.title: Infinite loop (try infinite-loop guardrail)
entries.code: while tt do (skip)
entries.blurb: Demonstrates the step-budget guardrail. Pasting this should NOT freeze the page.
